title: Recurrent Pregnancy Loss
chief_complaint: I've had 3 miscarriages all at 8-10 weeks over the past 2 years. All of them happened after seeing a heartbeat. I'm pregnant again at 7 weeks and I'm terrified I'm going to lose this one too.

physical_exam.Obstetric: Fundal height appropriate for 7 weeks. No vaginal bleeding. Cervix closed, long.
physical_exam.General: Anxious. Overweight (BMI 29). No stigmata of connective tissue disease.
physical_exam.Pelvic: Uterus slightly enlarged (consistent with early pregnancy). No adnexal masses.

hidden_data.ob_us: Intrauterine pregnancy at 7 weeks. Fetal HR 145 (normal). CRL appropriate for dates. No subchorionic hematoma.
hidden_data.rpl_workup: Karyotype both parents: Normal (46,XX and 46,XY). Uterine cavity: Hysteroscopy normal, no septum, no fibroids, no adhesions. Antiphospholipid antibodies: Lupus anticoagulant positive (×2 occasions 12 weeks apart), Anti-cardiolipin IgM positive, Anti-beta-2 glycoprotein IgM positive. TSH 3.5 (slightly elevated - aim <2.5 in pregnancy). Prolactin normal. HbA1c 5.5%. Thrombophilia panel: Factor V Leiden negative, Prothrombin mutation negative, Protein C/S normal.
hidden_data.current_pregnancy: Beta-hCG 45,000 at 7 weeks (appropriate). Progesterone 18 ng/mL (adequate - >15 supports pregnancy).

correct_diagnosis: Recurrent Pregnancy Loss from Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) - Treatment with Lovenox and Aspirin Indicated
key_learning_points: Recurrent pregnancy loss = ≥2-3 consecutive miscarriages (definitions vary). Affects 1-2% of couples trying to conceive. Etiology: Embryonic aneuploidy (50-60% - most common, random, increases with maternal age), Uterine anomalies (septum, fibroids, adhesions - 10-15%), APS (10-15%), Endocrinopathies (thyroid, diabetes, PCOS), Thrombophilias (controversial association), Parental balanced translocation (3-5%), Unexplained (50%)

APS = acquired thrombophilia causing arterial/venous thrombosis and/or pregnancy morbidity. Diagnosis (Sydney criteria): Clinical (≥1 pregnancy loss >10 weeks OR ≥3 losses <10 weeks OR preterm birth <34 weeks for severe preeclampsia/placental insufficiency) + Laboratory (Lupus anticoagulant, Anti-cardiolipin IgG/IgM, Anti-beta-2 GPI IgG/IgM - positive on 2 occasions >12 weeks apart)

APS management in pregnancy: Lovenox (LMWH) 40mg SC daily (prophylactic dose) + Low-dose Aspirin (LDA) 81mg daily. Start LDA preconception (or at positive pregnancy test). Start Lovenox at confirmation of intrauterine pregnancy. Continue through pregnancy and 6 weeks postpartum (highest risk of thrombosis is postpartum). Increases live birth rate from 20-50% to 70-80%

Prognosis without treatment: Live birth rate 20-50% with APS. With LDA + Lovenox: Live birth rate 70-80%. Recurrence despite treatment 20-30%. Additional therapies if refractory: Hydroxychloroquine, Prednisone (controversial), IVIG (limited evidence). Close surveillance: Fetal growth serial US, antenatal testing (NST/BPP starting 32 weeks), Preeclampsia surveillance

Other interventions for RPL (unexplained): Progesterone supplementation (controversial - some benefit if bleeding in early pregnancy), hCG support, Preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidy (PGT-A) if IVF, Treating chronic endometritis, Lifestyle: Smoking cessation, Limit caffeine <200mg/day, Optimize BMI, Prenatal vitamins with folic acid 400-800mcg (reduces neural tube defects). Psychological support: RPL is emotionally devastating - offer counseling and support groups
